data.title: ALX Oncology announces pricing of underwritten offering; shares up nearly 15%
data.description:
• ALX Oncology (ALXO) announced on Friday the pricing of an underwritten offering of common stock and pre-funded warrants.
  • The firm is selling 76,979,112 shares of common stock and, in lieu of common stock to certain investors, pre-funded warrants to purchase 18,574,120 shares of common stock in the offering.
  • The shares of common stock are being sold at an offering price of $1.57 per share, the closing price on January 29, 2026, and the pre-funded warrants are being sold at an offering price of $1.569 per pre-funded warrant, which represents the per-share offering price for each share of common stock.
  • The gross proceeds to ALX Oncology from this offering are expected to be approximately $150 million, before deducting the underwriting discounts and commissions and other estimated offering expenses, and excluding the exercise of any pre-funded warrants. A
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 2, 2026.
  • ALX Oncology anticipates using the net proceeds from the offering to fund the continued clinical development of evorpacept and its ALX2004 program and the related clinical trials and for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • ALXO +15% premarket to $1.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Western Digital rises after 'solid' Q2 and outlook as analysts turn more bullish
data.description:
Shares of Western Digital (WDC) rose about 3% premarket on Friday after fiscal second quarter results beat estimates, drawing positive reactions from analysts.

J.P. Morgan kept its Overweight rating on the memory maker while raising the price target on the stock to $320 from $175.

"The (very encouraging) writing was already on the wall following STX’s strong print earlier in the week, and right on cue, WDC delivered a solid beat-and-raise, echoing much of the commentary we heard from its chief competitor on pricing, exabyte growth and demand expectations. Perhaps more impressive than WD’s top-line performance (with Mar-Qtr guidance +7% vs. Street consensus) is the incremental margins that are materializing (>75% Q/Q in prior two quarters and implied ~70% for Mar-Qtr) as its customers aggressively transition to higher capacity drives," said analysts led by Harlan Sur.

The analysts noted that for now, the company's management is not resetting the bar on incremental margin expectations, but reaffirmed prior commentary of "more than 50%," which at this stage is a fairly low bar, but enough to take gross margin within striking distance of 50% exiting calendar year 2026, as per J.P. Morgan estimates.

The analysts added that visibility beyond 2026 continues to improve, with  Western Digital securing "robust commercial agreements" with two customers through 2027, and even one through 2028 — a clear indication that customers are unwilling to risk falling short on storage capacity (and potentially under-serving their own customers) as AI-related demand continues to march higher.

"Importantly, each of these long-term contracts bakes in both volume and pricing commitments, which gives WD visibility not just on EB [exabytes] demand, but also on the outlook for pricing, which looks set to grow at a LSD-MSD [low single digit/mid single digit] Y/Y% cadence for the next couple of years," said Sur and his team. 

The analysts noted that Western Digital's management characterized the timing of its HAMR production ramp as "the start of CY27." However, the analysts said that given qualification is already starting this month with one major customer (followed “relatively soon” by another), it would not surprise them if ramp timing is pulled forward to the fourth quarter of calendar 2026.

Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording, or HAMR, is a next-generation technology for hard disk drives.

Evercore maintained its Outperform rating on Western Digital's stock and increased the price target on the stock to 310 from $230.

"Another impressive quarter reinforces our view that WDC is in a structurally stronger position (we expect a more detailed LT roadmap at Innovation Day next week). The ongoing mix shift toward UltraSMR and HAMR should drive further gross margin expansion, and we see a clear path to ~$12.00 EPS by FY27," said analysts led by Amit Daryanani.

UltraSMR is Western Digital's advanced, high-capacity hard disk drive technology designed for data centers.

The analysts added that the company also disclosed a recent acquisition of laser-related intellectual property, or IP, assets, enabling greater vertical integration, improved manufacturability, and lower power requirements.

BofA reiterated its Buy rating on Western Digital citing secular growth of HDD market, strong EB growth driven by improved mix and further gross margin upside.

The firm raised the price target on the stock to $345 from $257.

"WDC reported F2Q26 earnings above expectations (GMs +770bps y/y, +220bps q/q) and guided above Street estimates on all metrics. F3Q guide implies continued rev/margin strength with revs of $3.1bn to $3.3bn," said analysts led by Wamsi Mohan. "Higher mix of UltraSMR is a tailwind to margins given its software-based architecture and 20% capacity uplift vs CMR."

Conventional Magnetic Recording, or CMR, is a traditional hard drive technology.

Wells Fargo maintained its Overweight rating and increased the price target on the shares to $335 from $260.

Sandisk (SNDK), which was spun off from Western Digital in February last year, also reported its quarterly results on Thursday which beat estimates. Shares of SanDisk surged about 22% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Chevron's Q4 oil production surges but earnings fall Y/Y on lower oil prices
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) -0.1% pre-market Friday after reporting Q4 adjusted earnings and revenues that fell from a year ago but came in above Wall Street expectations; shares closed the previous session at the highest since April 2023 and are up 12% this month.

Q4 net profit fell to $2.77B, or $1.39/share, from $3.24B, or $1.84/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues declined 10% Y/Y to $46.79B, primarily due to lower crude oil prices and severance costs and other transaction costs related to the Hess acquisition; Q4 adjusted profit totaled $3B, or $1.52/share, exceeding Wall Street expectations.

Q4 total production rose more than 20% Y/Y to 4.05M boe/day, and worldwide and U.S. net oil-equivalent production set annual records, with the Hess acquisition contributing 261K boe/day and legacy operations added another 124K boe/day, driven by growth in the Permian Basin and project ramp-ups at TCO and in the U.S. Gulf.

Q4 U.S. upstream earnings fell to $1.26B from $1.42B in the year-earlier quarter, primarily due to lower liquids realizations, partly offset by the impact of higher sales volumes and the absence of prior year severance charges, while Q4 International upstream earnings fell to $1.78B from $2.88B a year ago, primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency effects largely in Australia, lower affiliate earnings, and lower realizations, partly offset by earnings from legacy Hess, primarily Guyana, and lower operating expenses in part due to the absence of prior-year severance charges

Q4 U.S. downstream earnings swung to a profit of $230M from a $348M loss in the year-earlier quarter, primarily due to lower operating expenses, in part due to the absence of prior-year severance charges, higher margins on refined product sales, and lower impairments, while Q4 International downstream earnings rose to $593M from $100M a year ago, primarily due to higher margins on refined product sales and the absence of prior-year impairments, partially offset by less favorable foreign currency effects.

Chevron (CVX) said it expects production to grow by 7%-10% in 2026, excluding asset sales, boosted in part by projects in Guyana and the U.S. Gulf.

data.title: Coya Therapeutics raises $11.1M in private placement led by Dr. Reddy's
data.description:
• Coya Therapeutics (COYA) on Friday said it has agreed to sell about 2.52 million shares in a private placement priced at $4.40 per share, raising gross proceeds of approximately $11.1 million.
  • Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories, through a subsidiary, invested $10 million, while Greenlight Capital, the company’s largest institutional shareholder, invested $1.1 million. 
  • The clinical-stage biotechnology company said it plans to use the proceeds to accelerate technology transfer and scale-up manufacturing for low-dose IL-2 to support commercial readiness of its COYA 302 program.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 30, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • COYA +2.39% premarket to $4.71.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eli Lilly bid to expand Mounjaro label declined in EU
data.description:
An expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency ((EMA)), declined on Friday to endorse heart failure as a new indication for Mounjaro (tirzepatide), Eli Lilly’s (LLY) blockbuster diabetes/obesity drug.

The GIP/GLP-1 dual receptor agonist is currently used along with diet and physical activity in the EU for weight management in obese or overweight people with weight-related health conditions.

The Indiana-based drugmaker has sought a label expansion for the injectable to treat obese adults showing evidence of chronic heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF).

“EMA did not recommend that a separate indication should be granted for the treatment of HFpEF,” the regulator said following a review by its Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use ((CHMP)).

However, it agreed to update the Mounjaro label to include relevant data supporting the company’s marketing application for the HFpEF indication.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Wells Fargo, General Motors, Citigroup, Comcast, and more
data.description:

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Citigroup (C) and Valero (VLO) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Additional Dividend Information

For investors looking to gain further access to the dividend space, listed below are some popular dividend-focused ETFs that can be further analyzed:

Dividend ETFs: (VIG), (SCHD), (VYM), (DGRO), (SDY), (DVY), (IDV), (DGRW), (NOBL), (HDV), (RDVY), (VIGI), and (SDVY).

data.title: Saratoga Investment prices $100M 7.50% unsecured notes due 2031
data.description:
• Saratoga Investment (SAR) priced an underwritten public offering of $100M of 7.50% unsecured notes due February 6, 2031.
  • The notes will mature on February 6, 2031, and are redeemable at the company’s option on or after February 6, 2028.
  • Interest will accrue at 7.50% annually and be paid quarterly on February 28, May 31, August 31, and November 30, starting May 31, 2026.
  • The company granted underwriters an option to purchase up to an additional $15M of notes.
  • The notes are expected to list on the NYSE under the symbol “SAV” within 30 days of issuance.

data.title: Nasdaq futures slide as tech-led selloff looks to deepen; Trump picks new Fed chair
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Friday as investors continued to digest a wave of corporate earnings as the selloff looked to deepen. 

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) fell 0.7%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) declined 0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) dropped 0.5%.

Apple (AAPL) shares were largely flat in premarket trade after releasing its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results that were driven by record-breaking iPhone sales.

Visa (V) stock slid 1.6% after the payment network's fiscal Q1 2026 earnings and revenue topped the average analyst estimates. 

The producer price index came in hotter than expected in December.

U.S. President Donald Trump said he has selected Kevin Warsh to be the next Federal Reserve chair. 

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3.2 basis points to 4.27%, and the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) advanced 4.6 basis points to 4.90%. The shorter-end 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) edged higher to 3.57%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Sandisk Corporation (SNDK) +20.07%, Deckers Outdoor Corporation (DECK) +12.71%, and ResMed Inc. (RMD) +3.95%. Decliners included KLA Corporation (KLAC) -10.06%, Newmont Corporation (NEM) -8.61%, and Freeport-McMoRan Inc. (FCX) -6.33%.

data.title: Franklin Resources Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 beats by $0.15, revenue of $2.33B beats by $370M
data.description:
• Franklin Resources press release (BEN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.70 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $2.33B (+3.6% Y/Y) beats by $370M.
  • Total AUM was $1,684.0 billion at December 31, 2025, up $22.8 billion during the quarter.
  • Cash and cash equivalents and investments were $5.1 billion and, including the Company’s direct investments in consolidated investment products (“CIPs”), were $6.2 billion4 at December 31, 2025.

data.title: Novo Nordisk announces departure of China head
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) on Friday announced that Christine Zhou, the longtime head of its China operations, will leave the company by the end of March. Her last day at the company will be March 31, Novo (NONOF) said on its official WeChat account.

Zhou, Global Senior Vice President and China General Manager of Novo (NVO), served Eli Lilly (LLY) for nearly two decades before joining the Danish drugmaker in 2018. She led the weight loss drugmaker’s operations in Taiwan, Hong Kong, Macau, and mainland China.

China generated DKK 18.5B in net sales for the company in 2024, making up ~16% of its total sales with ~11% YoY growth. Zhou has overseen Novo’s (NVO) market rollouts of its GLP-1 blockbuster diabetes drug, Ozempic, and its obesity version, Wegovy, in China.

Her departure comes at a time when the company is engaging in a fierce battle with Eli Lilly (LLY) to improve sales of its GLP-1 products in China ahead of an upcoming patent cliff for semaglutide, the active ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy, in March.
